theme.title: Mixed / Stock-Picker's Market
theme.summary: Technology (+2.84%) and Energy (+0.78%) led the market today, while Consumer Staples (-0.94%) and Communication Svcs (-0.52%) brought up the rear. Growth sectors outpaced the rest, averaging +0.76% versus -0.17% for defensives and +0.21% for value, consistent with risk appetite favoring higher-beta names. Major indices moved in a tighter band — S&P 500 +0.89%, Dow +0.74%, Nasdaq +1.29% — without one clearly dominating. Small caps bucked the broader trend, with the Russell 2000 up +1.53%, outperforming large caps. The VIX fell 8.58% to 17.05, which reads as an orderly rotation rather than panic-driven selling. Gold added 0.83% to $4,084.60, crude oil was little changed at $84.79, and bitcoin gained 0.22% — cross-asset moves weren't all pointing the same direction as equities.
